title: QuantumBlaze ERP: Core Dashboard & General Navigation
description: General guide explaining the main operational metrics, sidebar collapsible options, global search, and notification panels.
content:
Welcome to QuantumBlaze ERP, the ultimate Ops Control center. This system is designed with a premium, high-speed, and reactive user interface built on Next.js and Tailwind CSS. Below is a comprehensive guide to the dashboard metrics, navigation panels, and interactive elements.

### Main Dashboard Overview
When you log into QuantumBlaze, you are presented with the Ops Control dashboard. It displays real-time key performance indicators (KPIs):
1. **Clients**: Total active clients linked to active contracts.
2. **Projects**: Number of ongoing software, design, and operations initiatives.
3. **Pending Invoices**: Total outstanding payments waiting for reconciliation.
4. **Active Tasks**: Checklist items assigned to teams across projects.

### The Global Sidebar
The navigation bar is situated on the left side and is split into logical business sections:
- **Core**: Contains the main Dashboard, Operations control, Intelligence Hub, and the AI Knowledge Base.
- **CRM**: Houses Leads tracking and the Clients registry.
- **Execution**: Tracks Projects management, real-time Chat, and shared Documents.
- **Finance**: Financial entries (Invoices, Receipts, Quotations) and Analytics visualization.
- **Workforce**: Unified HR operations including directories, leaves, shift plans, performance OKRs, recruitment, and payroll runs.

### Sidebar Actions
At the bottom of the sidebar, you will find footer actions:
- **Collapse Sidebar**: Toggle the sidebar width between 72px (icon-only mode) and 260px (full expanded view). This toggle is positioned as the top action in the footer.
- **System Settings**: Access settings, global configurations, and integrations. This link is placed at the very base of the sidebar.

### Search & System Controls
- **Global Search (Ctrl + K / Cmd + K)**: Located in the top header. Pressing the shortcut opens a rapid search modal. You can lookup any record by ID prefix (CLI-, PRJ-, INV-, TSK-) or names to jump instantly to their detail pages.
- **Notification Bell**: Click to view real-time system alerts like shift swap requests, candidate interview bookings, or client payment confirmations.
- **Profile Dropdown**: Access your personal profile details, efficiency rates, and log out securely.

title: Projects Management & Execution Pipelines
description: Guides users on setting up projects, creating milestones and tasks, configuring project teams, generating SRS documents, and managing proposals.
content:
The Execution module tracks ongoing project tasks, software development sprints, and client proposals. QuantumBlaze features an intelligent sequential ID engine that automatically structures project workflows.

### Creating a Project
To start a new project:
1. Open **Projects** from the sidebar and click the **+ New Project** button in the top right corner.
2. Select an active **Client** from the dropdown menu.
3. Enter the project name, overall budget, timeline deadlines, and select project managers.
4. QuantumBlaze automatically generates a unique project ID using the formula: `PRJ-[CLIENT_ABBR]-[YEAR]-[SEQ]`. For example: `PRJ-GOOG-26-004` (representing the 4th project for Google created in 2026).

### Project Detail Tabs
Once a project is created, click on it to open its management workspace. It is structured into 6 tabs:
1. **Overview**: Key metrics including budget burn rates, progress gauges, and visual timelines.
2. **Milestones**: Create project phases. Milestones are assigned IDs in the format `MST-[PRJ_ID]-[SEQ]` (e.g. `MST-PRJ-GOOG-26-004-01`).
3. **Tasks**: The active work checklist. Tasks generate automatic sequential IDs like `TSK-[PRJ_ID]-[SEQ]` (e.g. `TSK-PRJ-GOOG-26-004-02`). Each task includes title, description, priority, points estimation, and assignee.
4. **Team**: Manage developers, UI/UX designers, and QA engineers. Displays a visual layout of active project roles and interactive efficiency metrics.
5. **Documents (SRS)**: The central wiki. Allows drafting Software Requirement Specifications (SRS) documents which are assigned the ID `SRS-[PRJ_ID]` (e.g. `SRS-PRJ-GOOG-26-004`).
6. **Financials**: Tracks itemized project expenses, billing logs, and linked invoices.

### Agreement Proposals
Under the financials or project settings, managers can generate legal service contracts and pricing agreements. QuantumBlaze supports automated document versioning for agreements. The version sequential ID is structured as `AGR-[PRJ_ID]-V[VER_NUMBER]`. For example, `AGR-PRJ-GOOG-26-004-V1` tracks the initial version of the Google contract, which increments if amendments are saved.

title: CRM: Leads Pipeline & Clients Management
description: Explains how to capture leads, track pipeline stages, add custom notes, and convert leads to clients using the conversion wizard.
content:
The Customer Relationship Management (CRM) module is split into two primary areas: Leads and Clients. This module is optimized to funnel potential deals into active client relationships smoothly.

### Leads Tracking & Pipeline
Leads represent potential clients or sales opportunities. To manage leads:
1. Navigate to the **Leads** page.
2. Capture new leads by inputting their company name, contact person, estimated deal value, and source (e.g., referral, organic web, cold email).
3. Leads progress through standard pipelines: *New, In Discussion, Proposal Sent, Negotiation, Closed Won, Closed Lost*.
4. Each lead card contains a **Notes** section where sales reps can write custom background information (e.g., 'Needs project collaboration module, good product fit').

### Client Ingestion & Conversions
When a lead is won, you can convert them into a client using the interactive wizard:
1. Select a lead in the 'Closed Won' stage.
2. Click the **Convert to Client** button on the lead details card.
3. The conversion wizard launches, automatically copying over the company name, contact details, email, and telephone parameters.
4. Input the billing address, payment terms (default Net-30), and select the industry segment.
5. QuantumBlaze's ID Engine creates a unique client sequence identifier: `CLI-[COMP_ABBR]-[YEAR]-[SEQ]`. For example, a company named 'Amazon' converted as the 12th client in 2026 will receive the ID `CLI-AMZN-26-012`.
6. The lead record is closed, and a new client profile is created in the **Clients** registry, prefilled with historical sales communications and ready for project associations.

title: Finance Operations: Invoices, Receipts, and Financial Analytics
description: Guides users on creating invoices, receipts, and quotations, managing payment terms, and reading the interactive Analytics reports.
content:
The Finance module handles billing, client agreements, quotes, and visual ledger reconciliations. All financial items automatically generate strict sequence-based IDs for compliance audits.

### Creating Financial Entries
To create invoices, quotes, or receipts, navigate to **Finance** and click the corresponding action button:
1. **Invoices**: Generate billing invoices for active client milestones. Select the project and input line items (service rates, taxes, and discounts). QuantumBlaze applies default payment terms (like *Net-15, Net-30, Net-60*). The ID is automatically generated as `INV-YYMM-[SEQ]` (e.g., `INV-2605-0014` represents the 14th invoice created in May 2026).
2. **Quotations**: Issue formal pricing estimates to leads or clients. Quotations receive sequential IDs like `QTO-YYMM-[SEQ]` (e.g. `QTO-2605-0008`).
3. **Receipts**: Reconcile received client payments. When a client pays an invoice, mark it paid and generate a receipt. Receipts are saved with IDs like `RCT-YYMM-[SEQ]` (e.g., `RCT-2605-0014`), which links back to the original invoice for auditing.

### Financial Analytics
The **Analytics** page is a visual intelligence suite showcasing financial health:
- **Revenue Trends**: Interactive charts showing monthly billing, collection efficiency, and year-over-year growth.
- **Operational Expenses (OpEx)**: Pie charts showing breakdown of expenditures like hosting fees, payroll, hardware, and marketing.
- **Balance Sheets**: Quick statements of current cash assets, pending account receivables, and accounts payable.
- **Ledgers**: Audit logs showing raw transaction journals. Each journal entry is assigned an ID like `LED-YY-[SEQ]` (e.g., `LED-26-0089`) for accounting records.

title: Workforce & HR Operations Manual
description: Complete reference guide to the unified HR suite, including directories, shift plans, allowances, performance OKRs, recruitment, and payroll.
content:
The Workforce module houses QuantumBlaze's human resources features. It is divided into employee directories, shifts, payroll management, performance OKRs, and recruitment pipelines.

### Employee Profiles & Departments
The **HR → Employees** directory lists all active personnel. Add staff by clicking **+ Add Employee**. Fill in details such as department (ENGINEERING, DESIGN, PRODUCT, MARKETING, SALES, HR, FINANCE) and set system roles. QuantumBlaze generates structured employee IDs using the pattern `USR-[INITIALS]-[YEAR]-[SEQ]` (e.g., a user named 'Jane Doe' added as the 5th user in 2026 becomes `USR-JD-26-005`).

### Shift Scheduling & Swaps
For shift workers, managers can create shifts on the **Shifts** calendar:
1. Define Shift templates (e.g., Day Shift, Night Shift, Weekend Cover) with strict hours.
2. Assign employees to shifts. Shift assignment IDs are tracked internally.
3. **Shift Swaps**: Employees can request a shift swap with a co-worker. The system records the swap request, sends a notification to the manager, and updates the shift assignments dynamically once approved.

### Payroll Configurations & Runs
The **Payroll** module configures salary structures, allowances, and statutory rules:
- **Allowance Types**: Create recurring additions (e.g., Travel, Housing, Medical allowances).
- **Statutory Rules**: Set local rules (e.g., TAX, Employee Provident Fund, health deductions).
- **Payroll Batches**: Execute monthly payroll runs. Select the batch month, verify allowance and deduction computations for each employee, and run the calculation. Individual employee slips are logged securely as *payroll items*.

### Performance OKRs & Review Cycles
1. **Performance Goals**: Establish high-level employee goals (OKRs).
2. **Key Results**: Define quantitative sub-targets linked to a parent Goal.
3. **Review Cycles**: Set annual or quarterly self-evaluations and manager review flows to monitor employee growth and output metrics.

### Recruitment & Candidate Interviewing
- **Job Openings**: Create job posts with descriptions, salaries, and department links.
- **Candidates**: Track applicants through stages: *Applied, Phone Screen, Interviewing, Offered, Rejected*.
- **Interviews**: Schedule interviews, assign panel interviewers, and record scores using standard structured **Scorecards**.

title: AI & Intelligence Hub: Gemini Integrations, pgvector, and RAG
description: Explains how the AI Assistant and global Ask AI floating widget leverage Google Gemini and pgvector for contextual document chat.
content:
QuantumBlaze ERP features premium Artificial Intelligence powered by the **Google Gemini API** and **Postgres pgvector**. This guide details the underlying RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) infrastructure.

### Gemini Configuration
Before the AI features function, an administrator must save a Google Gemini API Key:
1. Go to **Settings → Integrations → Google Gemini AI**.
2. Paste your API key and select a base model (default is `gemini-1.5-flash` or newer models).
3. QuantumBlaze lists all available models dynamically. The configuration change is automatically timestamped on save.

### The Embedding Pipeline
When a document is added to the **Knowledge Base**:
1. The system splits the raw text into chunks of approximately 500 words. It uses a paragraph-boundary-aware JS splitter to ensure text context is preserved.
2. It triggers a REST API request to Google's highly efficient `text-embedding-004` model.
3. This outputs a **768-dimensional vector representation** of the chunk.
4. The vector is saved in the Neon Postgres `knowledge_chunks` table using pgvector's custom `vector(768)` type.

### Semantic Search & RAG Chat
When a user types a query into the floating **Ask AI widget** or the **AI Assistant Chat**:
1. QuantumBlaze immediately embeds the query string using `text-embedding-004` to get its 768-dimensional vector.
2. The system executes a high-speed cosine similarity query: `1 - (embedding <=> ?::vector) AS similarity`.
3. It fetches the top 4 chunks that score above a similarity threshold of **0.35**.
4. These chunks are assembled into a structured system prompt context along with the user's original question.
5. The base Gemini model (e.g. `gemini-1.5-flash`) processes the prompt and returns a grounded, contextual answer.
6. The chat window renders the response along with clickable inline citation chips displaying the matching document titles and similarity percentages.

title: GitHub Developer Suite: Setup, Configuration, and Workflows
description: Step-by-step administrator guide to creating a GitHub App, configuring webhooks, repository permissions, user OAuth, and development pipelines.
content:
The GitHub Developer Suite enables QuantumBlaze to automatically provision software repositories, sync branches and Pull Requests, register project team members as collaborators, and receive real-time commit/merge alerts.

### 1. Creating the GitHub App
To connect the ERP, you must create a dedicated GitHub App under your Organization settings:
1. Navigate to your GitHub Organization Developer Settings (e.g., `https://github.com/organizations/YOUR_ORG/settings/apps`) and click **New GitHub App**.
2. **App Name & Homepage**: Set a custom name (e.g., `QuantumBlaze ERP Suite`) and specify the domain URL of your ERP instance as the **Homepage URL**.

### 2. Configuring OAuth & Callback URL
1. In the **User authorization response (OAuth)** section, check the **Active** option.
2. Set the **Callback URL** to: `[YOUR_APP_URL]/api/auth/github/callback` (for local environments, use `http://localhost:3000/api/auth/github/callback`).
3. Check the box for **Request user authorization (OAuth) during installation** directly below the callback URL input.

### 3. Setting Up Webhooks & Local Development
1. In the **Webhook** section, check **Active**.
2. Set the **Webhook URL** to: `[YOUR_APP_URL]/api/webhooks/github`.
3. Create and record a secure **Webhook Secret** string.
4. **Local Development (localhost) Notice**: GitHub cannot deliver webhooks to local computers directly. 
   - *Recommended*: Generate a proxy channel via Smee.io, set the Smee URL as your Webhook URL, and run the forwarder locally: `npx -y smee-client -u [SmeeURL] -p 3000 -P /api/webhooks/github`.
   - *Alternative*: Disable webhooks by unchecking the 'Active' option. App API calls (repository setup, branch loading, Pull Request generation) will still function 100%.

### 4. Custom App Permissions
In the **Permissions & events** left sidebar, configure the following granular access permissions:
- **Repository Permissions**:
  - *Administration*: Read & Write (required to setup repos and configure webhooks)
  - *Contents*: Read & Write (required to read branches and draft pull request templates)
  - *Issues*: Read & Write
  - *Pull Requests*: Read & Write (required to launch new PRs from ERP)
  - *Metadata*: Read-only (automatically selected by GitHub)
  - Set all other repository options to *No access*.
- **Organization Permissions**:
  - *Members*: Read-only (required to sync developer accounts)
  - Set all other organization options to *No access*.
- **Account Permissions**: Set all options to *No access*.

### 5. Webhook Event Subscriptions
At the bottom of the **Permissions & events** page, subscribe to exactly these webhook events:
1. **Issues**
2. **Label**
3. **Member**
4. **Pull request**
5. **Push**
6. **Repository**

### 6. Installation Target Scope & Creating the App
1. Under **Where can this GitHub App be installed?**, select **Any account** to ensure multi-tenant compatibility.
2. Click the green **Create GitHub App** button.

### 7. Credentials Generation & Syncing with ERP Settings
Once created, gather the necessary settings to configure the integration under **Settings → Integrations → GitHub Developer Suite**:
1. Copy the **App ID** and **Client ID** from the App main page.
2. Click **Generate a new client secret** in the Client Secrets block and copy the key immediately.
3. Scroll to the bottom and click **Generate a private key**. Open the downloaded `.pem` file, and copy the entire RSA Private Key string including headers.
4. Go to **Install App** in the left sidebar, click Install next to your target Organization, and complete the GitHub installation.
5. Input these parameters in the ERP configurations dashboard under **Organization / Owner Name** along with the gathered keys, and save. All development pipelines are now fully active!
